title.text: Tag, copy and move ideas and posts

description.text: Moving input between phases and projects.

body:0002.text: There are many reasons why you might want to reuse or highlight contributions, some examples are:

body:0003.text: Present winners in a new phase after jury evaluation

body:0004.text: Prepare a pre-selection for citizens to vote on

body:0005.text: Showcase high-quality ideas in later stages

body:0006.text: Copying and moving input helps carry ideas forward in the project timeline or into other projects.

body:0007.text: ⚠️ : Moving input doesn’t create a new idea, the same input simply appears in multiple phases. If you edit it later, the change applies everywhere it appears. This also means that if you move an input to another project it doesn't live in both projects, it only lives in the one it was moved to.

body:0009.text: The Input Manager exists at two levels:

body:0011.text: Project-level: Ideation, proposals, or voting phase → Input Manager

body:0013.text: Default view is Timeline, showing which phase inputs belong to

body:0014.text: Numbers below contributions indicate assigned phases

body:0015.text: Drag-and-drop bulk actions let you assign up to 10 inputs at once

body:0017.text: Proposal submissions do not appear in the platform-level Input Manager

body:0018.text: Proposals can only be managed through the project-level Input Manager

body:0019.text: How to move or copy inputs between phases?

body:0020.text: Inputs can be moved between ideation phases (phases using the Collect input and feedback or Proposals, petitions or initiatives method, or voting phases ( phases using the Conduct a voting or prioritization exercise method).

body:0021.text: Ensure your targeted phases are using the Collect input and feedback or Proposals, petitions or initiatives OR Conduct a voting or prioritization exercise methods.

body:0022.text: In the Admin panel, open the project and select the phase where the current inputs is in

body:0024.text: In the Timeline view, select the input you want to copy or move.

body:0025.text: To copy: keep the input in multiple phases by selecting multiple blue number bubbles below the input, which corresponds to the phases you want the input in

body:0026.text: To move: remove it from the original phase and add it to another by de-selecting the current blue number bubble and select the correct blue number bubble below the input, which corresponds to the phases you want the input in.

body:0027.text: To unassign input and remove it from all phases: deselect all blue number bubble below the input. The input moves to the All Phases tab until reassigned.

body:0028.text: To bulk move up to 10 inputs: select them and click and drag to the desired phase listed on the left.

body:0029.text: How to add tags to inputs?

body:0030.text: Step 1: go to the Input Manager of the phase where you want to add tags to your inputs

body:0031.text: Step 2: go to the Tags section of the Input Manager

body:0032.text: Step 3: select the inputs that you want to assign a tag to by clicking the square box

body:0033.text: Step 4: drag-and-drop any of the selected inputs to a tag and all the selected inputs will be assigned this tag

body:0034.text: How to move input between projects?

body:0036.text: Step 2: Drag and drop the input onto the destination project.

body:0037.text: ℹ️ : Drag‑and‑drop only works this way: inputs → projects (not projects → inputs).

body:0038.text: Step 3: Manually assign the input to the right phase in the new project:

body:0039.text: Go to the new project via the Projects tab.

body:0040.text: Click the correct phase number under the input to place it.

body:0041.text: If you don’t assign a phase, it defaults to All Phases and won’t show up in any phase view.

body:0043.text: The URL of the contribution doesn’t change when it moves between projects.

body:0044.text: Inputs from proposals can not be moved to ideation projects and vice versa.
